---
title: "Fire Department Preparedness Dashboard"
subtitle: "GLHLTH 562 Final Project"
title-slide-attributes:
  class: title-slide
format:
  revealjs:
    theme: [simple]
    css: theme.css
    slide-number: true
    transition: fade
    incremental: true
---

![](assets/helmet.svg){.slide-icon}

## Problem Addressed

- Firefighter fatalities (LODDs) are **largely preventable**
- Fire department leaders need **actionable guidance** to improve training and incident planning
- Existing data is scattered across large datasets and not tailored to specific department characteristics, making it **inaccessible** to most fire leadership
- This product combines USFA fatality data with Census and FEMA context **into practical prevention and preparedness guidance.**

![](assets/helmet.svg){.slide-icon}

## Live Demo / Walkthrough

- Select region or state, department makeup, and incident types.
- Review regional risk summary and personnel patterns.
- Generate prevention guidance (LLM) and a 12-month training plan
- Explore classifications from your incident narratives
- Explore disaster preparedness guidance

![](assets/helmet.svg){.slide-icon}

## Key Design Decisions & Trade-Offs

- **Fatalities, not injuries** volume of data vs. quality and standard reporting
- **Included disaster data** after noticing large fatality spikes around historical disasters
- **Formatting of graphs and charts** legible for 10 - 1,000 data points
- **Outputs structured into sections** for clarity & separate purposes
- **Simple models and interpretations** to make accessible for audience

![](assets/helmet.svg){.slide-icon}

## What I Would Improve With More Time

- Consider incorporation of additional data sources (NFIRS/NERIS) for incident volume, civilian fatalities, etc.
- Expand validation of LLM outputs
- Add role-specific training plans
- Validate with more target users
